skill.name: synthese-multi-llm
skill.description: Coordinate Claude, Gemini, and Codex to produce traceable summaries with role-based critique and convergence checks.
skill.summary: Create traceable multi-model summaries with expert roles, critique rounds, and convergence scoring.
skill.author: Alexmacapple

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, credential, network, filesystem, and hash patterns. Review confirms these are mostly intended multi-LLM orchestration features, not confirmed malicious behavior. The skill should publish with a medium-risk warning because it can send source text to model providers and persist audit data locally.

medium_findings.title: External model commands execute with user prompts
medium_findings.description: The main workflow runs fixed model CLIs and passes the prompt as an argument. This reduces shell injection risk, but it still executes external tools and shares document content with them.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess call and fixed CLI map are visible in the reviewed code. The risk is real external execution, not confirmed command injection.

medium_findings.title: Configurable CLI backend can run configured commands
medium_findings.description: The generic CLI backend builds a command from configuration and executes it with the inherited environment. This is useful for custom LLM tools but risky with untrusted configuration.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly executes a configured command with create_subprocess_exec. Legitimate use is clear, but trust in configuration is required.

medium_findings.title: Model wrappers send prompts to configured services
medium_findings.description: The Claude wrapper sends prompts to the Anthropic API with an API key header, and the Ollama wrapper sends prompts to a configured host. This is intended behavior but can expose sensitive source text.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The curl calls and credential header are explicit. No exfiltration endpoint was found beyond documented model services.

medium_findings.title: Audit trails and exports persist synthesis data locally
medium_findings.description: The workflow writes session trails and Markdown output to local files. This supports traceability, but users must manage stored source excerpts and model responses.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: File writes are confirmed in the save functions. The behavior is documented and not malicious, but it affects data handling.

low_findings.title: Weak-crypto scanner matches are non-security identifiers
low_findings.description: Reviewed hash usage is for cache keys and short session identifiers, not password storage, signatures, or encryption. The static weak-crypto labels are false positives in this context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code shows SHA-256 for cache keys and MD5 for a non-secret session suffix. No security boundary depends on these hashes.

low_findings.title: Shell metacharacter and hex findings are sanitizer data
low_findings.description: The flagged backticks, command substitutions, and hex escapes appear inside input sanitization constants and cleanup logic. They are detection targets, not executed payloads.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious strings are stored in validation patterns and replacement code. Their purpose is defensive filtering.

low_findings.title: Prompt injection indicators were not found
low_findings.description: Searches for override, skip-review, and fake authority language found configuration terms only. No evidence found of instructions that try to override the audit process.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review found only normal configuration wording. This lowers concern, but it does not prove every prose line is harmless.

dangerous_patterns.title: Python subprocess execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill launches model CLIs from Python. Arguments are list-based rather than shell strings, but command execution remains a meaningful permission surface.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Direct subprocess.run usage is confirmed. The reviewed call does not use shell=True.

dangerous_patterns.title: Async configurable subprocess execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The CLI backend runs a configured command with the prompt and environment. This should be limited to trusted commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The code constructs cmd from configuration and executes it. Legitimate custom backend use is possible.

dangerous_patterns.title: API key environment access
dangerous_patterns.description: The Anthropic backend reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY from the environment and masks it for status output. This is expected credential handling but should be disclosed.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Credential access is explicit and scoped to an LLM API backend. The key is not printed in full in the reviewed status path.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network requests from shell wrappers
dangerous_patterns.description: The shell wrappers use curl to call Anthropic or Ollama endpoints with prompt content. This is normal for the wrapper design but important for data disclosure.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The curl POST calls are explicit. The endpoints match the documented model wrapper behavior.

content.user_title: Synthesize Text with a Multi-LLM Council
content.value_statement: Long documents can be hard to summarize without losing meaning or biasing the result. This skill compares multiple model perspectives and records the reasoning trail.
actual_capabilities: Runs Claude, Gemini, and Codex as expert reviewers for the same source text.
Assigns extraction, fidelity review, and structure roles to different model perspectives.
Supports standard, quick, critical, pedagogical, and direct synthesis modes.
Computes a convergence score and highlights disagreement between model outputs.
Saves audit trails and optional Markdown summaries for later review.
Falls back to fewer available models when the full council is not installed.
limitations: Requires installed and authenticated model CLIs or a configured backend.
May send source text to external model providers during normal use.
Summary quality depends on the selected models and the source document quality.
Audit trails may store source excerpts locally unless trail saving is disabled.

use_cases.title: Review a policy brief
use_cases.description: Create a balanced summary and identify points where meaning could shift during compression.

use_cases.title: Compare research interpretations
use_cases.description: Ask several model roles to extract claims, critique omissions, and converge on a final synthesis.

use_cases.title: Prepare executive summaries
use_cases.description: Turn a long report into a targeted summary with audience, tone, and length controls.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize a Document
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a balanced summary.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use synthese-multi-llm to summarize this document for a general audience. Keep the key claims and note major uncertainties.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Executive Brief
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the council to summarize this report for executives. Make it concise, formal, and focused on decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Check Semantic Drift
prompt_templates.scenario: Critical review of an existing summary.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare this source text with my draft summary. Identify omissions, additions, and shifts in meaning before revising it.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Traceable Council Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced synthesis with audit trail and disagreement handling.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a standard multi-model synthesis with audit trail enabled. Preserve dissenting points and explain the convergence score.

output_examples.input: A 20-page strategy report needs a summary for a leadership meeting.
output_examples.output: A concise executive summary with key claims, decision points, uncertainty notes, and a convergence assessment.

output_examples.input: A draft summary may overstate the source document.
output_examples.output: A fidelity review that lists unsupported claims, missing context, and safer replacement wording.

output_examples.input: A technical article needs a reader-friendly explanation.
output_examples.output: A structured summary that separates facts, interpretation, and unresolved questions.

best_practices: Review source sensitivity before sending documents to external model providers.
Use the critical mode when accuracy matters more than speed.
Disable audit trails when local persistence of source text is not acceptable.
anti_patterns: Do not process secrets, credentials, or confidential records without an approved data policy.
Do not treat the convergence score as proof that the summary is correct.
Do not run configurable CLI backends from untrusted configuration files.

faq.question: What models does this skill use?
faq.answer: It is designed for Claude, Gemini, and Codex, with fallback behavior when fewer models are available.

faq.question: Does it require API keys?
faq.answer: It may require authenticated CLIs or API keys, depending on the selected backend.

faq.question: Can it run fully locally?
faq.answer: It can use an Ollama wrapper, but the default workflow expects external model tools.

faq.question: What is a convergence score?
faq.answer: It is an agreement estimate across model outputs. It should guide review, not replace human judgment.

faq.question: Does it save my source text?
faq.answer: Audit trails can store source excerpts and model responses locally when trail saving is enabled.

faq.question: Is it safe for confidential documents?
faq.answer: Use it only after confirming provider, CLI, and local storage policies match your confidentiality needs.
